content: Comparative evaluations of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) against standard curious agents can be reframed as a study of how an overarching 'agent of agents' meta-system tunes its own active learning memory retention to prioritize high-uncertainty exploration. This meta-agent would retain performance gaps between curiosity methods as tagged uncertainty markers, updating its own intrinsic reward signal to explore more environments where existing curiosity strategies diverge in outcomes.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
